title: Insider Trade: Director acquires 50,000 shares at ₹2,450
summary: Mukesh D. Ambani, Chairman & Managing Director, acquired 50,000 equity shares of Reliance Industries at an average price of ₹2,450 per share through market purchase on 25-Mar-2026. Total transaction value: ₹12.25 Cr. Post-transaction holding: 45.23%.

Price per Share: ₹2,450.00
Transaction Type: Market Purchase
Date of Transaction: 25-Mar-2026
Post Transaction Holding: 45.23%
Mode of Acquisition: Market Purchase

title: DRHP Filed: Swiggy plans ₹10,000 Cr IPO with fresh issue of ₹5,000 Cr
summary: Swiggy Limited has filed its Draft Red Herring Prospectus with SEBI for an IPO comprising a fresh issue of equity shares aggregating to ₹5,000 crore and an Offer for Sale of up to ₹5,000 crore. The company reported revenue of ₹8,264 Cr in FY26 with a net loss narrowing to ₹612 Cr from ₹2,350 Cr in FY24.
raw_text:
DRAFT RED HERRING PROSPECTUS
Issuer: Swiggy Limited
Fresh Issue: ₹5,000 Crore
Offer for Sale: ₹5,000 Crore
Total Issue Size: ₹10,000 Crore
Revenue FY26: ₹8,264 Crore
Net Loss FY26: ₹612 Crore
Gross Margin: 28.4%
Monthly Active Users: 52 Million
Key Risk: Continued operating losses, intense competition from Zomato

title: Promoter Pledge: 8.2% of shares pledged by Adani family trust
summary: S.B. Adani Family Trust has pledged 3.2 Cr equity shares (8.2% of total promoter holding) of Adani Enterprises Ltd with Punjab National Bank as collateral for a ₹15,000 Cr loan facility. Total promoter pledge now stands at 17.4% of promoter holding.

Reason for Pledge: Collateral for loan facility
Lender: Punjab National Bank
Loan Amount: ₹15,000 Crore
Total Promoter Pledge After Transaction: 17.4%
Date of Pledge: 24-Mar-2026

title: Bulk Deal: Goldman Sachs acquires 1.2% stake worth ₹2,800 Cr
summary: Goldman Sachs Singapore PTE acquired 4.5 Cr shares (1.2% stake) of Tata Motors at ₹622 per share in a bulk deal on NSE. Total deal value: ₹2,799 Cr. This is Goldman's first significant position in Tata Motors in 3 years.

title: Board Meeting: Special dividend of ₹32/share + buyback of ₹12,000 Cr announced
summary: Infosys Board has approved a special dividend of ₹32 per share and a share buyback of ₹12,000 Crore via open market route. Record date for dividend: 10-Apr-2026. Buyback price cap: ₹1,800 per share. Board also revised FY27 revenue guidance upward to 4-6% CC growth.
raw_text:
Board Meeting Outcome - Infosys Limited
Date: 25-Mar-2026
Key Decisions:
1. Special Dividend: ₹32 per equity share (face value ₹5)
2. Share Buyback: ₹12,000 Crore via Open Market route
3. Buyback Price Cap: ₹1,800 per share
4. Record Date: April 10, 2026
5. FY27 Revenue Guidance: 4-6% CC Growth (revised upward from 1-3%)
6. Operating Margin Guidance: 21-23%

title: Q3 FY26 Results: PAT up 22% YoY to ₹17,250 Cr, NIM expansion of 15bps
summary: HDFC Bank reported Q3 FY26 net profit of ₹17,250 Cr (+22% YoY, +8% QoQ). Net Interest Margin expanded 15bps YoY to 3.65%. Gross NPA at 1.24% (vs 1.34% QoQ). Loan growth at 18.2% YoY. Provisions declined 12% QoQ indicating improving asset quality.
raw_text:
Quarterly Results - HDFC Bank Limited
Quarter: Q3 FY26 (Oct-Dec 2025)
Net Profit: ₹17,250 Crore (+22% YoY)
Net Interest Income: ₹31,420 Crore (+19% YoY)
NIM: 3.65% (+15bps YoY)
Gross NPA: 1.24% (vs 1.34% previous quarter)
Net NPA: 0.31%
Credit Growth: 18.2% YoY
Deposit Growth: 21.4% YoY
Provisions: ₹2,850 Crore (-12% QoQ)
ROA: 2.05%
ROE: 17.8%

title: FII Holding: Foreign investors increase stake to 42.3% — highest in 5 years
summary: SEBI shareholding pattern filing shows FII/FPI holding in ITC increased to 42.3% in Q3 FY26, up from 38.1% in Q2 FY26. This represents the highest foreign ownership in 5 years. Domestic mutual fund holding declined from 18.2% to 15.8% in the same period.
raw_text:
Shareholding Pattern - ITC Limited
Quarter: Q3 FY26
Promoter: 0.00% (unchanged)
FII/FPI: 42.3% (up from 38.1%)
Domestic MF: 15.8% (down from 18.2%)
DII: 22.1% (down from 24.5%)
Retail: 19.8% (up from 19.2%)
Change in FII: +4.2% — largest quarterly increase since 2021
Notable FII buyers: Vanguard, BlackRock, GIC Singapore

title: Stock Split: 1:5 split approved, face value from ₹10 to ₹2
summary: Bajaj Finance has approved a stock split in the ratio of 1:5 (face value from ₹10 to ₹2 per share). Record date: 15-Apr-2026. Current price ~₹7,200/share implies post-split price of ~₹1,440. Board cited improving retail participation as rationale.
raw_text:
Corporate Action - Bajaj Finance Limited
Date of Announcement: 25-Mar-2026
Action Type: Stock Split
Split Ratio: 1:5
Face Value Before: ₹10
Face Value After: ₹2
Record Date: April 15, 2026
Board Resolution Date: March 25, 2026
Rationale: Improve liquidity and retail participation
Current Market Price: ₹7,200 (approx.)
Estimated Post-Split Price: ₹1,440 (approx.)
